page: Amazon Web Services c6in.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores based on the x86_64 Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. The system includes 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage.

This instance is highly optimized for networking, delivering a baseline network bandwidth of 150 Gbps. The Intel Xeon processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 (specifically avx512f and avx512_vnni) for vector processing, alongside cryptographic acceleration features such as AES, VAES, and SHA_NI.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and Geekbench multi-core scores placing in the top 25% tier. Cryptographic performance is particularly notable, with the Passmark CPU encryption test reaching the top 10% tier. Single-core performance for both stress-ng and Geekbench falls in the middle 50% tier. Memory bandwidth tests, including large block read and write operations, consistently rank in the top 25% tier.

Given the high network throughput of 150 Gbps and strong multi-core processing, this instance is designed for network-intensive compute workloads, distributed databases, and static web serving. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio represents a compute-heavy resource density, offering qualitative cost efficiency for parallel processing tasks that do not require massive memory footprints or local NVMe storage.
description: Amazon Web Services c6in.24xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory and offers a baseline network bandwidth of 150 Gbps, though it lacks local storage and GPUs. Benchmark results show strong multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and top-tier cryptographic execution, while single-core metrics remain in the average tier. The processor supports AVX-512 and hardware-accelerated cryptography. This instance is designed for network-intensive compute workloads, static web serving, and database operations where high-speed networking and parallel processing are required.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6in.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and 150 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong multi-core and cryptographic performance.

192.0 GB of DDR4 memory with a 2.0 GB per core ratio
